title: How does final expense insurance work?
description: Covers funeral costs and other end-of-life expenses with guaranteed payouts.
callout: Final expense is a small whole life policy designed specifically to pay for funeral and related costs when you pass away.

content.text: **Final expense insurance operates as a streamlined whole life insurance policy with coverage amounts typically ranging from $5,000 to $40,000. Unlike traditional life insurance that focuses on income replacement, final expense insurance addresses the immediate financial burden that families face when someone passes away.**

items: **Application**: Simple health questionnaire, no medical exam required
**Premium Payments**: Level monthly payments that never increase
**Death Benefit**: Tax-free payout goes directly to your chosen beneficiary
**Claim Process**: Typically processed within 7-14 days of submitting death certificate

content.text: 💡 **Key Insight**: The average funeral costs $8,000-$12,000, but final expense policies provide flexibility to use funds for any immediate needs.

variations: What does final expense coverage include?

title: Can I get coverage if I have health issues?
description: Yes—simplified underwriting makes coverage accessible even with health conditions.
callout: Final expense insurance uses simplified underwriting, making it one of the most accessible types of life insurance for people with health issues.

content.text: **The beauty of final expense insurance lies in its accessibility. While traditional life insurance often requires extensive medical exams and can exclude people with various health conditions, final expense policies are designed with simplified underwriting that opens the door for many who have been previously declined.**

items: **Simplified Issue**: 5-10 basic health questions, no medical exam
**Guaranteed Issue**: No health questions at all, automatic acceptance
**Modified Benefits**: 2-3 year waiting period for full benefits with guaranteed issue

content.text: 🎯 **Success Story**: Even clients with diabetes, heart conditions, or previous cancer diagnoses often qualify for immediate coverage through simplified issue policies.

variations: Can I get final expense with diabetes?
